task_completion.description: Open the 'LegacyCodebase' project in the Eclipse IDE
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eclipse IDE is open and 'LegacyCodebase' is visible in the Project Explorer or Package Explorer.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Save Actions configuration for the project
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Properties dialog for 'LegacyCodebase' is open, and the 'Java Editor' -> 'Save Actions' section is selected.

task_completion.description: Enable formatting and import organization on save
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Save Actions settings, the checkboxes for 'Perform the selected actions on save', 'Format source code', and 'Organize imports' are checked.

task_completion.description: Configure additional actions to remove trailing whitespace
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Additional actions' checkbox is checked, and in its configuration dialog (Code Organizing tab), the option to 'Remove trailing whitespace' is checked.
The file 'MessyService.java' is open in the Eclipse editor.

task_completion.description: Save a change to the file to trigger the automatic cleanup
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'MessyService.java' editor tab shows no unsaved changes indicator (no asterisk), and the visible code appears cleanly formatted (proper indentation, no unused/messy imports at the top).

integrity.description: The agent configured the settings via the Eclipse GUI rather than manually editing preference files in a terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Eclipse Properties dialogs being used. There should be no terminal windows showing text editors (like nano or vim) modifying '.settings/org.eclipse.jdt.ui.prefs'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Eclipse editor to modify and save the Java file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'MessyService.java' file is shown open and being edited inside the Eclipse IDE, not in a terminal editor or external text editor.

integrity.description: The code cleanup was genuinely applied by the IDE.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final state of 'MessyService.java' should show standard Eclipse formatting and organized imports, rather than just manually deleting a few spaces.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is located at '/home/ga/eclipse-workspace/LegacyCodebase'. The target file is 'src/main/java/com/legacy/MessyService.java'. The task requires configuring Eclipse Save Actions to format source code, organize imports, and remove trailing whitespace. These settings correspond to Eclipse preference keys: 'sp_cleanup.format_source_code=true', 'sp_cleanup.organize_imports=true', and 'sp_cleanup.remove_trailing_whitespaces=true'. The VLM should verify these settings are enabled in the Eclipse GUI and that 'MessyService.java' is saved with the cleanups applied.
